channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Iran's oil as part of the interim agreement
item.description: The U.S. on Monday lifted sanctions on Iranian oil as part of the interim agreement.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. on Monday lifted sanctions on Iranian oil as part of the interim agreement.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Former national security advisor on $300B Iran reconstruction fund in U.S. agreement
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with former National Security Advisor Jake Sullivan about the financial component of the U.S. memorandum of understanding with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with former National Security Advisor Jake Sullivan about the financial component of the U.S. memorandum of understanding with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: As Republican Party looks to future without Trump in office, Utah could be a road map
item.description: In 2028, President Trump will not be on the ballot, leaving Republicans to decide the future of the party. Utah — which has a complicated relationship with the president — could be a starting point.
item.content:encoded:
In 2028, President Trump will not be on the ballot, leaving Republicans to decide the future of the party. Utah — which has a complicated relationship with the president — could be a starting point.

(Image credit: Rick Egan)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Here are the nonfiction books NPR staffers have loved so far this year
item.description: A biography of Hannibal Lecter. A meditation on trees. A memoir by a child prodigy violinist. A treatise on the way we poop. These are just a few of the nonfiction books our NPR colleagues are enjoying.
item.content:encoded: A biography of Hannibal Lecter. A meditation on trees. A memoir by a child prodigy violinist. A treatise on the way we poop. These are just a few of the nonfiction books our NPR colleagues are enjoying.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Collins Sullivan

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. lifts oil sanctions on Iran, Trump visits Pennsylvania to tout economic wins, judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Minnesota officials.
item.content:encoded: U.S. lifts oil sanctions on Iran, Trump visits Pennsylvania to tout economic wins, judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Minnesota officials.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 'Toy Story 5' puts a spotlight on screen time for kids
item.description: "Toy Story 5" brought in more than $160 million domestically over its first weekend. The movie, however, raises issues about how much screen time is too much for kids.
item.content:encoded: "Toy Story 5" brought in more than $160 million domestically over its first weekend. The movie, however, raises issues about how much screen time is too much for kids.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Fans around the world are creating their own World Cup anthems
item.description: Forget the official World Cup album. Soccer fans around the globe are coming up with their own songs to support their teams.
item.content:encoded: Forget the official World Cup album. Soccer fans around the globe are coming up with their own songs to support their teams.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Andy Burnham, a former mayor, could become the U.K.'s next prime minister
item.description: The former mayor of Manchester, England, Andy Burnham, may soon replace British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation. Burnham would be the U.K.'s seventh leader in 10 years.
item.content:encoded: The former mayor of Manchester, England, Andy Burnham, may soon replace British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation. Burnham would be the U.K.'s seventh leader in 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: How Britain's economic troubles toppled another prime minister
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Idrees Kahloon of The Atlantic about Britain's economic decline and the political consequences that compelled another prime minister to resign.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Idrees Kahloon of The Atlantic about Britain's economic decline and the political consequences that compelled another prime minister to resign.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Israel's prime minister faces pressure internationally and at home
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is suffering one of his biggest political crises, publicly admonished by his ally President Trump while Israelis say they are ready for change at the top.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is suffering one of his biggest political crises, publicly admonished by his ally President Trump while Israelis say they are ready for change at the top.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn
